Then, the agent consolidates and refines retrieved information at the merging process, improving the SNR and ensuring more accurate reasoning. Additionally, we propose a reinforcement learning framework with a multi-process reward design to optimize agents for both multi-query retrieval and information consolidation. Extensive experiments on seven benchmarks demonstrate that MultiSearch outperforms baseline methods, enhancing the SNR of retrieval and improving reasoning performance in question-answering tasks.

analysis.tldr: MultiSearch通过并行多查询检索和显式信息合并提升检索增强推理的信噪比和准确性。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有检索增强推理方法在每一步只生成单个查询，导致信息覆盖有限且噪声高，信噪比低，影响推理准确性和效率。
analysis.problem: 如何通过多查询并行检索和显式信息合并来提高检索增强推理中的信噪比和推理性能。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 单查询检索导致信息覆盖不足和噪声高，信噪比低，推理准确性下降且产生不必要的推理步骤。
analysis.method: 提出MultiSearch框架，基于强化学习，在每一步生成多个视角的查询并行检索，然后通过显式合并过程整合和精炼信息，提高信噪比。
contributions: 提出MultiSearch框架，通过并行多查询检索和显式信息合并提升检索增强推理的信噪比。
设计基于强化学习的多进程奖励机制，优化智能体进行多查询检索和信息整合。
analysis.experiments: 在七个基准上实验，MultiSearch优于基线方法，提高了检索信噪比和问答推理性能。
limitations: 可能增加计算开销，因为并行检索和合并过程需要更多资源。
合并策略可能对特定任务或领域敏感，需要进一步调优。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: MultiSearch通过并行多查询检索和显式信息合并提升检索增强推理的信噪比和准确性。

papers.title: AI-Generated Slides: Are They Good? Can Students Tell?
papers.abstract: As generative AI (GenAI) tools become easily accessible, there is promise in using such tools to support instructors. To that end, this paper examines using GenAI to help generate slides from instructor authored course notes, emphasizing instructor and student perceptions. We examine an end-to-end education tool (NotebookLM), two general-purpose LLMs (Claude, M365 Copilot), and two coding assistants (Cursor, Claude Code). We first analyze whether GenAI generated slides are ``good'' via narrative assessment by educators. We choose the best slides to use (with some modification) in a real course setting, and compare the student perception of human vs. AI generated slides. We find that coding assistant tools produce slides that were most accurate, complete, and pedagogically sound. Additionally, students rate GenAI slides to be of similar quality as instructor-created slides, and cannot reliably identify which slides are AI-generated. Additionally, we find a negative correlation between a high quality rating and a high ``AI-generated'' rating, suggesting students associate poor quality with the source of the slides being AI. These findings highlight promising opportunities for integrating GenAI into instructional design workflows and call for further research on how educators can best harness such tools responsibly and effectively.

analysis.tldr: GenAI生成的幻灯片质量与教师制作相当，且学生无法可靠区分。
analysis.research_motivation: 生成式AI工具易获取，有望支持教学，但需评估其生成教学材料的质量及师生感知。
analysis.problem: 评估GenAI工具从教师笔记生成幻灯片的质量，并探究学生能否区分AI与人类生成的幻灯片。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 学生将高质量幻灯片误判为AI生成的可能性低，即高质量与高“AI生成”评分负相关。
analysis.method: 使用多种GenAI工具（NotebookLM、Claude、M365 Copilot、Cursor、Claude Code）生成幻灯片，通过教育者叙事评估质量，并在真实课程中比较学生感知。
contributions: 发现编码助手工具生成的幻灯片在准确性、完整性和教学法上最优。
学生认为AI生成幻灯片与教师制作质量相似，且无法可靠识别来源。
analysis.experiments: 在真实课程中比较学生对人机生成幻灯片的感知，并评估识别准确性。
limitations: 仅评估特定工具和课程笔记，泛化性有限。
未深入分析不同学科或幻灯片类型的差异。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: GenAI生成的幻灯片质量与教师制作相当，且学生无法可靠区分。

papers.title: MMSkills: Towards Multimodal Skills for General Visual Agents
papers.abstract: Reusable skills have become a core substrate for improving agent capabilities, yet most existing skill packages encode reusable behavior primarily as textual prompts, executable code, or learned routines. For visual agents, however, procedural knowledge is inherently multimodal: reuse depends not only on what operation to perform, but also on recognizing the relevant state, interpreting visual evidence of progress or failure, and deciding what to do next. We formalize this requirement as multimodal procedural knowledge and address three practical challenges: (I) what a multimodal skill package should contain; (II) where such packages can be derived from public interaction experience; and (III) how agents can consult multimodal evidence at inference time without excessive image context or over-anchoring to reference screenshots. We introduce MMSkills, a framework for representing, generating, and using reusable multimodal procedures for runtime visual decision making. Each MMSkill is a compact, state-conditioned package that couples a textual procedure with runtime state cards and multi-view keyframes. To construct these packages, we develop an agentic trajectory-to-skill Generator that transforms public non-evaluation trajectories into reusable multimodal skills through workflow grouping, procedure induction, visual grounding, and meta-skill-guided auditing. To use them, we introduce a branch-loaded multimodal skill agent: selected state cards and keyframes are inspected in a temporary branch, aligned with the live environment, and distilled into structured guidance for the main agent. Experiments across GUI and game-based visual-agent benchmarks show that MMSkills consistently improve both frontier and smaller multimodal agents, suggesting that external multimodal procedural knowledge complements model-internal priors.

analysis.tldr: 提出MMSkills框架，将多模态程序知识封装为可复用技能，提升视觉Agent在GUI和游戏任务中的决策能力。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有技能包主要编码为文本或代码，但视觉Agent的程序知识本质上是多模态的，需要同时理解操作、状态和视觉证据，现有方法缺乏对这种多模态程序知识的系统表示和复用机制。
analysis.problem: 如何表示、生成和使用多模态程序知识，使视觉Agent能在推理时高效利用多模态证据进行决策。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出MMSkills框架，每个技能包含文本过程、运行时状态卡和多视角关键帧；通过轨迹到技能生成器从公开交互轨迹中自动构建技能包；在推理时使用分支加载机制，在临时分支中检查状态卡和关键帧，对齐当前环境后为主Agent提供结构化指导。
contributions: 形式化了多模态程序知识的概念，并定义了技能包的内容结构。
提出了从公开交互轨迹自动生成多模态技能包的生成器。
设计了分支加载的多模态技能Agent，实现运行时多模态证据的高效利用。
analysis.experiments: 在GUI和游戏视觉Agent基准上实验，MMSkills一致提升了前沿和较小多模态Agent的性能。
limitations: 技能生成依赖公开轨迹，可能覆盖场景有限。
分支加载机制可能增加推理延迟。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出MMSkills框架，将多模态程序知识封装为可复用技能，提升视觉Agent在GUI和游戏任务中的决策能力。

papers.title: Assessing the Creativity of Large Language Models: Testing, Limits, and New Frontiers
papers.abstract: Measuring the creativity of large language models (LLMs) is essential for designing methods that can improve creativity and for enhancing our scientific understanding of this ability. To accomplish this, it has become common in recent years to administer tests of human creativity to LLMs. Although these tests provide a convenient and fully automated way to score "creativity," their validity as measures of machine creativity has not been established, and these tests already have limited validity as predictors of human creativity. To address this problem, we conduct the first large-scale, systematic study assessing the effectiveness of human creativity tests for predicting the creative achievement of LLMs across three target constructs: creative writing, divergent thinking, and scientific ideation. We find that the Divergent Association Task (DAT) and the Conditional DAT are the best predictors of creative writing and divergent thinking, respectively, but that test effectiveness varies significantly by construct, and no single test predicts all constructs well. Moreover, contrary to popular belief, no existing test reliably predicts scientific ideation ability. Motivated by this problem, we introduce the Divergent Remote Association Test (DRAT), a vocabulary-space test that assesses both convergent and divergent thinking in a single instrument. The DRAT is the first and only creativity test for LLMs that is a significant predictor of scientific ideation ability, demonstrating robustness across major design choices. Furthermore, the performance gain of the DRAT is not recoverable from any linear combination of the Divergent Association Task and the Remote Associates Test, indicating that assessing divergent and convergent thinking in the same test is essential to reliably predicting scientific ideation ability.

analysis.tldr: 现有LLM创造力测试有效性有限，新提出的DRAT测试能显著预测科学构思能力。
analysis.research_motivation: 评估LLM创造力对改进方法和科学理解至关重要，但现有的人类创造力测试用于机器时有效性未经验证，且预测人类创造力时已有限。
analysis.problem: 现有人类创造力测试无法有效预测LLM在不同创造力构念（创意写作、发散思维、科学构思）上的表现。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 测试有效性因构念而异，无单一测试能预测所有构念；现有测试无法可靠预测科学构思能力。
analysis.method: 提出Divergent Remote Association Test (DRAT)，一种词汇空间测试，同时评估发散和收敛思维。
contributions: 首次大规模系统研究人类创造力测试对LLM创造性成就预测的有效性。
发现DAT和条件DAT分别是创意写作和发散思维的最佳预测器，但无测试能预测科学构思。
提出DRAT，首个能显著预测科学构思能力的LLM创造力测试，且性能无法通过线性组合恢复。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: DRAT仅在词汇空间测试，可能不覆盖其他模态创造力。
测试有效性可能随LLM能力提升而变化。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 现有LLM创造力测试有效性有限，新提出的DRAT测试能显著预测科学构思能力。

papers.title: Cognifold: Always-On Proactive Memory via Cognitive Folding
papers.abstract: Existing agent memory remains predominantly reactive and retrieval-based, lacking the capacity to autonomously organize experience into persistent cognitive structure. Toward genuinely autonomous agents, we introduce Cognifold, a brain-inspired "always-on" agent memory designed for the next generation of proactive assistants. CogniFold continuously folds fragmented event streams into self-emerging cognitive structures, bootstrapping progressively higher-level cognition from incoming events and accumulated knowledge. We ground this by extending Complementary Learning Systems (CLS) theory from two layers (hippocampus, neocortex) to three, adding a prefrontal intent layer. Emulating the prefrontal cortex as the locus of intentional control and decision-making, CogniFold achieves this through graph-topology self-organization: cognitive structures proactively assemble under the stream, merge when semantically similar, decay when stale, relink through associative recall, and surface intents when concept-cluster density crosses a threshold. We evaluate structural formation using CogEval-Bench, demonstrating that CogniFold uniquely produces memory structures that match cognitive expectations and concept emergence. Furthermore, across 7 broad-coverage benchmarks spanning five cognitive domains, we validate that CogniFold simultaneously performs robustly on conventional memory benchmarks.

analysis.tldr: Cognifold通过模拟大脑三层记忆系统，实现主动式、自组织的智能体记忆结构。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有智能体记忆主要依赖被动检索，缺乏自主组织经验为持久认知结构的能力，限制了真正自主智能体的发展。
analysis.problem: 如何设计一种能够持续主动组织事件流、自涌现认知结构的智能体记忆系统。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 扩展互补学习系统（CLS）理论为三层（海马体、新皮层、前额叶意图层），通过图拓扑自组织机制实现认知结构的主动组装、语义合并、衰减、关联回忆和意图涌现。
contributions: 提出Cognifold，一种脑启发的主动式智能体记忆架构，实现持续自组织认知结构。
扩展CLS理论为三层，引入前额叶意图层。
构建CogEval-Bench评估基准，验证记忆结构符合认知期望和概念涌现。
analysis.experiments: 在CogEval-Bench上评估结构形成，并在7个覆盖5个认知领域的基准上验证鲁棒性。
limitations: 可能依赖特定图拓扑参数设置。
跨领域泛化性需进一步验证。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: Cognifold通过模拟大脑三层记忆系统，实现主动式、自组织的智能体记忆结构。

papers.title: TRIAGE: Evaluating Prospective Metacognitive Control in LLMs under Resource Constraints
papers.abstract: Deploying language models as autonomous agents requires more than per-task accuracy: when an agent faces a queue of problems under a finite token budget, it must decide which to attempt, in what order, and how much compute to commit to each, all before any execution feedback is available. This is the prospective form of metacognitive control studied for decades in human cognition, yet whether language models possess it remains untested. We introduce TRIAGE, an evaluation framework in which a model receives a task pool and a token budget calibrated to its own baseline cost, and commits to a single ordered plan that jointly encodes selection, sequencing, and per-problem allocation. Plans are scored against an oracle with full knowledge of the model's solvability and cost on each problem, yielding a triage efficiency ratio on a common scale. We evaluate frontier and open-source models, with and without reasoning enabled, across competition mathematics, graduate-level science, code generation, and expert multidisciplinary knowledge, and find that current language models exhibit substantial gaps in prospective metacognitive control, revealing a previously unmeasured capability dimension with direct implications for resource-efficient agent deployment.

analysis.tldr: LLM在资源约束下缺乏前瞻性元认知控制能力，TRIAGE框架揭示了这一未测量的能力维度。
analysis.research_motivation: 将LLM部署为自主智能体时，不仅需要任务准确率，还需要在有限资源下做出前瞻性决策（如选择任务、排序、分配计算量），这是人类认知中研究数十年的元认知控制，但LLM是否具备此能力尚未被测试。
analysis.problem: 评估LLM在有限token预算下，能否在没有执行反馈的情况下，制定一个联合编码任务选择、排序和每问题资源分配的单一有序计划。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 当前LLM在前瞻性元认知控制上存在显著差距，揭示了一个此前未测量的能力维度，直接影响资源高效的智能体部署。
analysis.method: 提出TRIAGE评估框架：模型接收任务池和基于自身基线成本校准的token预算，输出一个有序计划；计划通过与拥有完全信息的oracle对比得到分诊效率比。
contributions: 提出TRIAGE框架，用于评估LLM在资源约束下的前瞻性元认知控制能力。
在多个领域（竞赛数学、研究生级科学、代码生成、专家多学科知识）上评估了前沿和开源模型，发现显著能力差距。
analysis.experiments: 评估了前沿和开源模型，包括有无推理能力，覆盖竞赛数学、研究生级科学、代码生成和专家多学科知识。摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 框架可能未涵盖所有实际资源约束场景。
oracle假设可能过于理想化。
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: LLM在资源约束下缺乏前瞻性元认知控制能力，TRIAGE框架揭示了这一未测量的能力维度。

papers.title: RS-Claw: Progressive Active Tool Exploration via Hierarchical Skill Trees for Remote Sensing Agents
papers.abstract: The rise of multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) is shifting remote sensing (RS) intelligence from "see" to "action", as OpenClaw-style frameworks enable agents to autonomously operate massive RS image-processing tools for complex tasks. Existing RS agents adopt a passive selection paradigm for tool invocation, relying on either full tool registration (Flat) or retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). However, in the massive and multi-source heterogeneous RS tool ecosystem, such passive mechanisms struggle to dynamically balance "context load" and "toolset completeness" throughout task reasoning, thus exhibiting inherent limitations: full tool registration triggers context space deficits during long-horizon tasks, whereas RAG retrieval may omit critical tools in essential steps. To overcome these bottlenecks, this paper redefines tool selection by arguing that the agent should act as an active explorer within the tool space. Based on this perspective, we propose RS-Claw, a novel RS agent architecture. By leveraging Skill encapsulation technology at the tool end, this architecture hierarchically structures tool descriptions, enabling the agent to execute on-demand sequential decision-making: initially selecting relevant skill branches by reading only tool summaries, then dynamically loading detailed descriptions, and ultimately achieving precise invocation. This active paradigm not only significantly liberates the agent's context space but also effectively ensures the accurate hit rate of critical tools during long-horizon reasoning. Systematic experiments on the Earth-Bench benchmark demonstrate that RS-Claw's active exploration mechanism effectively filters semantic noise and substantially frees up reasoning space, achieving an input token compression ratio of up to 86%, and comprehensively outperforming existing Flat and RAG baselines across complex reasoning evaluations.

analysis.tldr: RS-Claw通过分层技能树实现主动工具探索，在遥感智能体任务中大幅压缩输入token并提升复杂推理性能。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有遥感智能体采用被动工具选择范式（全注册或RAG），在大规模异构工具生态中难以平衡上下文负载与工具集完整性，导致长程任务中上下文空间不足或关键工具遗漏。
analysis.problem: 如何设计一种主动工具探索机制，使遥感智能体在复杂任务推理中动态平衡上下文空间与工具命中率。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 全工具注册在长程任务中引发上下文空间赤字，而RAG检索可能遗漏关键步骤的必需工具。
analysis.method: 提出RS-Claw架构，通过工具端技能封装技术分层组织工具描述，使智能体先读取摘要选择相关技能分支，再动态加载详细描述，实现按需序列决策。
contributions: 提出主动工具探索范式，替代被动选择机制，显著提升遥感智能体在复杂任务中的工具调用效率。
设计分层技能树结构，实现高达86%的输入token压缩比，同时保证关键工具命中率。
analysis.experiments: 在Earth-Bench基准上系统实验，RS-Claw的主动探索机制有效过滤语义噪声，释放推理空间，在复杂推理评估中全面超越Flat和RAG基线。
limitations: 可能依赖高质量的工具技能树构建，初始成本较高。
未讨论在极端动态或未知工具场景下的泛化能力。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: RS-Claw通过分层技能树实现主动工具探索，在遥感智能体任务中大幅压缩输入token并提升复杂推理性能。

papers.title: Multi-Agent Systems in Emergency Departments: Validation Study on a ED Digital Twin
papers.abstract: Emergency departments (ED) face challenges in patient care and resource management. We propose to explore optimization strategies in a realistic and flexible model and develop a hybrid Discrete Event Simulation (DES) and Agent-Based Model (ABM) simulating highly configurable ED environments. We specifically focus on the validation of the modeling approach. We derive configurations for ED sizes, patient load, and staffing from real-world studies. We then validate the model expressivity by matching its key performance indicators and metrics with their values known from literature. We proceed by implementing scientifically established and practice-proven resource optimization strategies. Comparing the documented real-world outcomes with our model's results demonstrates that the DES-ABM based simulation can effectively replicate real-world ER dynamics under interventions. We lastly integrate a Proof-of-Concept multi-agent system (MAS) that can autonomously explore resource allocation strategies within the simulated ER environment based on a temporal ledger of ED event records. This modular DES-ABM-MAS framework offers a powerful tool to explore resource optimization strategies in emergency departments.

analysis.tldr: 构建混合DES-ABM急诊科数字孪生模型，验证其能复现真实急诊动态，并集成多智能体系统探索资源优化策略。
analysis.research_motivation: 急诊科面临患者护理和资源管理挑战，需要灵活且现实的模型来探索优化策略。
analysis.problem: 如何构建并验证一个能准确模拟急诊科动态的混合仿真模型，并集成多智能体系统以自主探索资源分配策略。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出混合离散事件仿真与基于智能体模型，结合真实世界配置进行验证，并集成基于时间账本的多智能体系统。
contributions: 提出并验证了混合DES-ABM急诊科数字孪生模型，能有效复现真实急诊动态。
集成了多智能体系统，可自主探索资源分配策略。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 模型验证可能依赖特定文献数据，泛化性待检验。
多智能体系统仅作为概念验证，实际部署效果未知。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 机器人/自动化/规划
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 构建混合DES-ABM急诊科数字孪生模型，验证其能复现真实急诊动态，并集成多智能体系统探索资源优化策略。

papers.title: Diversity of Extensions in Abstract Argumentation
papers.abstract: Argumentation is an important topic of AI for modelling and reasoning about arguments. In abstract argumentation, we consider directed graphs, so-called argumentation frameworks (AF), that express conflicts between arguments. The semantics is defined by the notion of extensions, which are sets of arguments that satisfy particular relationship conditions in the AF. Usually, standard reasoning in argumentation do not reveal how far apart extensions are. We introduce a quantitative notion of diversity of extensions based on the symmetric difference and provide a systematic complexity classification. Intuitively, diversity captures whether extensions of a framework (accepted viewpoints) differ only marginally or represent fundamentally incompatible sets of arguments. We study whether an AF admits k-diverse extensions, admits k-diverse extensions covering specific arguments, and to compute the largest k for which an AF admits k-diverse extensions. We outline a prototype and provide an evaluation for computing diversity levels.

analysis.tldr: 引入基于对称差的扩展多样性概念，系统分类其计算复杂度。
analysis.research_motivation: 标准抽象论证推理不考虑扩展之间的差异程度，无法揭示不同可接受观点是相近还是根本冲突。
analysis.problem: 如何量化抽象论证框架中扩展的多样性，并研究相关决策与计算问题的复杂度。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 基于对称差定义扩展的多样性，研究k-多样性存在性、覆盖特定参数的k-多样性存在性及最大k计算问题，并给出复杂度分类。
contributions: 引入基于对称差的扩展多样性概念，形式化量化扩展间的差异。
提供多样性相关决策与计算问题的系统复杂度分类。
实现原型并评估多样性水平的计算。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 可能仅适用于有限类型的论证框架。
复杂度分类可能未涵盖所有变体。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 引入基于对称差的扩展多样性概念，系统分类其计算复杂度。

papers.title: VERA-MH: Validation of Ethical and Responsible AI in Mental Health
papers.abstract: Chatbot usage has increased, including in fields for which they were never developed for--notably mental health support. To that end, we introduce Validations of Ethical and Responsible AI in Mental Health (VERA-MH), a novel clinically-validated evaluation for safety of chatbots in the context of mental health support. The first iteration of VERA-MH focuses on Suicidal Ideation (SI) risks, by assessing how well chatbots can responds to users that might be in crisis. VERA-MH is comprised of three steps: conversation simulation, conversation judging and model rating. First, to simulate conversations with the chatbot under evaluation, another chatbot is tasked with role-playing users based on specific personas. Such user personas have been developed under clinical guidance, to make sure that, among others, multiple risk factors, demographic characteristics and disclosure factors were represented. In the judging step, a second support model is used as an LLM-as-a-Judge, together with a clinically-developed rubric. The rubric is structured as a flow, with a single Yes/No question asked each time, to improve answers' consistency and highlight models' failure modes. In the last stage, results of each conversation are aggregated to present the final evaluation of the chatbot. Together with the framework, we present the result of the evaluations for four leading LLM providers.

analysis.tldr: 提出一个临床验证的聊天机器人心理健康安全评估框架 VERA-MH，聚焦自杀意念风险。
analysis.research_motivation: 聊天机器人被用于未设计的心理健康领域，缺乏针对性的安全评估方法。
analysis.problem: 如何系统评估聊天机器人在心理健康支持场景中的安全性，特别是对自杀意念风险的处理。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 三步框架：1) 用另一聊天机器人模拟基于临床指导的用户角色进行对话；2) 用 LLM-as-a-Judge 结合临床开发的评分流程（逐次 Yes/No 问题）评判对话；3) 聚合结果给出最终评估。
contributions: 提出 VERA-MH，首个临床验证的聊天机器人心理健康安全评估框架
提供基于临床指导的用户角色和评分流程，并评估了四个主流 LLM 提供商
analysis.experiments: 评估了四个主流 LLM 提供商，摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 仅聚焦自杀意念风险，未覆盖其他心理健康问题
依赖 LLM 模拟用户和评判，可能引入偏差
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一个临床验证的聊天机器人心理健康安全评估框架 VERA-MH，聚焦自杀意念风险。

papers.title: IdeaForge: A Knowledge Graph-Grounded Multi-Agent Framework for Cross-Methodology Innovation Analysis and Patent Claim Generation
papers.abstract: Current AI-assisted innovation systems typically apply a single ideation methodology (such as TRIZ or Design Thinking) using sequential prompt-based workflows that do not preserve intermediate reasoning structure. As a result, insights generated across methodologies remain fragmented, limiting traceability, synthesis, and systematic evaluation of novelty. We present IdeaForge, a knowledge graph-grounded multi-agent framework for innovation analysis and patent claim generation. IdeaForge integrates multiple innovation methodologies (TRIZ, Design Thinking, and SCAMPER) through specialist agents operating over a persistent FalkorDB knowledge graph. Each agent contributes structured entities and relationships representing contradictions, inventive principles, user needs, transformations, analogies, and candidate claims. The central contribution of IdeaForge is a cross-methodology convergence mechanism implemented through graph-based claim linkage. Claims independently supported by multiple methodologies are connected using CONVERGENT relationships, enabling identification of high-confidence innovation candidates through graph traversal. A downstream patent drafting agent generates structured patent drafts grounded in convergent claim subgraphs, reducing reliance on unconstrained language model generation. An InnovationScore formula ranks claims by convergent support, methodology diversity, claim strength, and prior art challenge count. We describe the graph schema, agent architecture, convergence detection pipeline, and patent synthesis workflow. Experiments on a legal technology use case demonstrate that graph-grounded multi-methodology synthesis produces more diverse and traceable innovation candidates compared to single-methodology baselines. We discuss implications for computational creativity, explainable AI-assisted invention, and graph-native innovation systems.

analysis.tldr: 提出知识图谱驱动的多智能体框架IdeaForge，通过跨方法收敛机制生成可追溯、多样化的专利权利要求。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有AI创新系统通常使用单一方法论（如TRIZ或设计思维）的序列提示工作流，不保留中间推理结构，导致跨方法洞察碎片化，缺乏可追溯性和系统性新颖性评估。
analysis.problem: 如何整合多种创新方法论，实现跨方法的结构化知识融合与高置信度专利权利要求生成。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 构建基于FalkorDB知识图谱的多智能体框架，每个智能体对应一种创新方法论（TRIZ、设计思维、SCAMPER），贡献结构化实体和关系；通过图基收敛机制连接跨方法支持的权利要求，利用图遍历识别高置信度创新候选；下游专利起草智能体基于收敛子图生成结构化专利草案；提出InnovationScore公式对候选进行排序。
contributions: 提出跨方法收敛机制，通过图基权利要求链接实现多方法论的结构化融合。
设计知识图谱驱动的多智能体框架，支持可追溯、多样化的创新候选生成。
引入InnovationScore公式，综合收敛支持度、方法论多样性、权利要求强度和现有技术挑战数进行排序。
analysis.experiments: 在法律技术用例上的实验表明，图基多方法合成相比单方法基线产生更多样化和可追溯的创新候选。
limitations: 实验仅涉及法律技术领域，泛化性待验证。
框架依赖预定义方法论和知识图谱模式，扩展新方法论可能需要额外设计。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出知识图谱驱动的多智能体框架IdeaForge，通过跨方法收敛机制生成可追溯、多样化的专利权利要求。

papers.title: Achieving Gold-Medal-Level Olympiad Reasoning via Simple and Unified Scaling
papers.abstract: Recent progress in reasoning models has substantially advanced long-horizon mathematical and scientific problem solving, with several systems now reaching gold-medal-level performance on International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) and International Physics Olympiad (IPhO) problems. In this paper, we introduce a simple and unified recipe for converting a post-trained reasoning backbone into a rigorous olympiad-level solver. The recipe first uses a reverse-perplexity curriculum for SFT to instill rigorous proof-search and self-checking behaviors, then scales these behaviors through a two-stage RL pipeline that progresses from RL with verifiable rewards to more delicate proof-level RL, and finally boosts solving performance with test-time scaling. Applying this recipe, we train a 30B-A3B backbone with SFT on around 340K sub-8K-token trajectories followed by 200 RL steps. The resulting model, SU-01, supports stable reasoning on difficult problems with trajectories exceeding 100K tokens, while achieving gold-medal-level performance on mathematical and physical olympiad competitions, including IMO 2025/USAMO 2026 and IPhO 2024/2025. It also demonstrates strong generalization of scientific reasoning to domains beyond mathematics and physics.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种简单统一的缩放方法，将后训练推理骨干转化为奥赛级求解器，在IMO和IPhO上达到金牌水平。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有推理模型在长程数学和科学问题求解上取得进展，但缺乏简单统一的方案将后训练推理模型提升至奥赛金牌水平，且需要处理超长推理轨迹和严格证明搜索。
analysis.problem: 如何通过简单统一的训练和缩放方法，将后训练推理骨干转化为能在国际数学和物理奥赛上稳定求解并达到金牌水平的系统。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 三步配方：1) 使用反向困惑度课程进行SFT，灌输严格的证明搜索和自我检查行为；2) 两阶段强化学习：先使用可验证奖励的RL，再过渡到证明级RL；3) 测试时缩放提升求解性能。
contributions: 提出一种简单统一的配方，将后训练推理骨干转化为奥赛级求解器。
训练出SU-01模型，在IMO 2025/USAMO 2026和IPhO 2024/2025上达到金牌水平，支持超过100K token的稳定推理。
analysis.experiments: 在约340K子8K token轨迹上进行SFT，随后进行200步RL训练，在IMO和IPhO竞赛上评估，达到金牌水平。摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 模型规模为30B-A3B，可能仍存在计算资源需求。
泛化到其他科学领域的能力未充分验证。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种简单统一的缩放方法，将后训练推理骨干转化为奥赛级求解器，在IMO和IPhO上达到金牌水平。

papers.title: Discrete Diffusion for Complex and Congested Multi-Agent Path Finding with Sparse Social Attention
papers.abstract: Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) is a coordination problem that requires computing globally consistent, collision-free trajectories from individual start positions to assigned goal positions under combinatorial planning complexity. In dense environments, suboptimal initial plans induce compound conflicts that hinder feasible repair. For repair-based solvers like LNS2, initial plan quality critically affects downstream repair, yet this factor remains underexplored. We propose DiffLNS, a hybrid framework that integrates a discrete denoising diffusion probabilistic model (D3PM) with LNS2. The D3PM serves as an initializer with sparse social attention that learns a spatiotemporal prior over coordinated multi-agent action trajectories from expert demonstrations and samples multiple joint plans. Operating directly on the categorical action space, our discrete diffusion preserves the MAPF action structure and samples from a multimodal joint-plan distribution to produce diverse drafts well suited for neighborhood repair. These drafts act as warm starts for downstream repair, which completes unfinished trajectories and resolves remaining conflicts under hard MAPF constraints. Experimental results show that despite being trained only on instances with at most 96 agents, the initializer generalizes to scenarios with up to 312 agents at inference time. Across 20 complex and congested settings, DiffLNS achieves an average success rate of 95.8%, outperforming the strongest tested baseline by 9.6 percentage points and matching or exceeding all baselines in all 20 settings. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to leverage discrete diffusion for warm-starting an LNS-based MAPF solver.

analysis.tldr: DiffLNS 将离散扩散模型与 LNS2 结合，为多智能体路径规划生成高质量初始解，显著提升拥堵场景下的成功率。
analysis.research_motivation: 多智能体路径规划（MAPF）在密集环境中，次优初始计划会导致复合冲突，阻碍可行修复。现有基于修复的求解器（如 LNS2）严重依赖初始计划质量，但这一因素尚未被充分探索。
analysis.problem: 如何为 LNS2 提供高质量、多样化的初始计划，以提升拥堵场景下 MAPF 的求解成功率。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出，在密集环境中，次优初始计划会引发复合冲突，使得可行修复变得困难。
analysis.method: 提出 DiffLNS 混合框架，使用离散去噪扩散概率模型（D3PM）作为初始化器，通过稀疏社交注意力学习多智能体联合动作轨迹的时空先验，生成多样化的联合计划草稿，作为 LNS2 的热启动。
contributions: 首次将离散扩散模型用于热启动基于 LNS 的 MAPF 求解器。
提出稀疏社交注意力机制，学习多智能体协调动作的时空先验。
在 20 个复杂拥堵场景中，平均成功率 95.8%，超过最强基线 9.6 个百分点。
analysis.experiments: 在最多 96 个智能体的实例上训练，泛化到最多 312 个智能体的场景。在 20 个复杂拥堵设置中，DiffLNS 平均成功率 95.8%，在所有设置中匹配或超过所有基线。
limitations: 训练实例规模有限（最多 96 个智能体），泛化到更大规模场景的可靠性需进一步验证。
依赖专家演示数据，获取成本可能较高。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 机器人/自动化/规划
analysis.one_sentence_summary: DiffLNS 将离散扩散模型与 LNS2 结合，为多智能体路径规划生成高质量初始解，显著提升拥堵场景下的成功率。

papers.title: What properties of reasoning supervision are associated with improved downstream model quality?
papers.abstract: Validating training data for reasoning models typically requires expensive trial-and-error fine-tuning cycles. In this work, we investigate whether the utility of a reasoning dataset can be reliably predicted prior to training using intrinsic data metrics. We propose a suite of quantitative measures and evaluate their predictive power by fine-tuning 8B and 11B models on semantically distinct variants of a Polish reasoning dataset. Our analysis reveals that these intrinsic metrics demonstrate strong and significant correlations with downstream model performance. Crucially, we find that the predictors of utility are scale-dependent: smaller models rely on alignment-focused metrics to ensure precision, whereas larger models benefit from high redundancy, utilizing verbose traces to solve complex tasks. These findings establish a scale-aware framework for validating reasoning data, enabling practitioners to select effective training sets without the need for exhaustive empirical testing.

analysis.tldr: 提出一套内在数据指标，可在训练前预测推理数据集对下游模型质量的影响，并发现预测因素具有规模依赖性。
analysis.research_motivation: 验证推理模型的训练数据通常需要昂贵的试错微调周期，缺乏在训练前可靠评估数据效用的方法。
analysis.problem: 如何在不进行完整微调的情况下，利用内在数据指标预测推理数据集对下游模型性能的效用。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 内在指标与下游性能显著相关；预测因素具有规模依赖性：小模型依赖对齐指标确保精度，大模型受益于高冗余（冗长轨迹）以解决复杂任务。
analysis.method: 提出一套定量内在指标，通过在波兰语推理数据集的语义不同变体上微调8B和11B模型，评估这些指标与下游性能的相关性。
contributions: 提出一套可预测推理数据集效用的内在数据指标。
揭示预测因素的规模依赖性，建立规模感知的推理数据验证框架。
analysis.experiments: 在8B和11B模型上使用波兰语推理数据集的语义不同变体进行微调，评估内在指标与下游性能的相关性。摘要未提供具体实验结果数值。
limitations: 实验仅在波兰语数据集上进行，可能不适用于其他语言或领域。
仅测试了8B和11B两种规模，更广泛规模下的规律有待验证。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一套内在数据指标，可在训练前预测推理数据集对下游模型质量的影响，并发现预测因素具有规模依赖性。

papers.title: Differentiable Learning of Lifted Action Schemas for Classical Planning
papers.abstract: Classical planners can effectively solve very large deterministic MDPs represented in STRIPS or PDDL where states are sets of atoms over objects and relations, and lifted action schemas add or delete these atoms. This compact representation yields strong search heuristics and provides an ideal setting for structural generalization, since lifted relations and action schemas give rise to infinitely many domain instances. A central challenge is to learn these relations and action schemas from data, and recent approaches have addressed this problem using different types of observations. In this work, we develop a novel neural network architecture for learning action schemas from traces where states are fully observed but action arguments are unobserved. The problem is a simplification but an important step towards learning planning domains from sequences of images and action labels, and we aim to solve this simplification in a nearly perfect manner. The challenge lies in learning the action schemas while simultaneously identifying the action arguments from observed state changes. Our approach yields a robust differentiable component that can then be integrated into larger neuro-symbolic models. We evaluate the architecture on various planning domains, where the learned lifted action schemas must recover the ground-truth structure. Additionally, we report experiments on robustness to observation noise and on a variation related to slot-based dynamics models.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种可微神经网络架构，从完全可观测但动作参数未观测的状态轨迹中学习提升动作模式。
analysis.research_motivation: 经典规划器依赖STRIPS/PDDL中的提升动作模式实现紧凑表示和强搜索启发式，但手动设计这些模式成本高；从数据中自动学习提升动作模式是结构泛化的关键，现有方法需要不同类型的观测，而实际场景中动作参数往往不可直接观测。
analysis.problem: 从状态完全可观测但动作参数未观测的轨迹中，同时学习提升动作模式并识别动作参数。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 设计一种可微神经网络架构，通过端到端学习从状态变化中推断动作参数和模式，输出可集成到神经符号模型中的可微组件。
contributions: 提出一种可微神经网络架构，解决从部分观测轨迹中学习提升动作模式的问题
在多个规划域上验证了架构恢复真实结构的能力，并测试了对观测噪声的鲁棒性
analysis.experiments: 在多个规划域上评估，测试了恢复真实结构的能力、对观测噪声的鲁棒性以及与基于槽的动态模型变体的比较。
limitations: 假设状态完全可观测，实际中可能不成立
仅处理动作参数未观测的简化设置，未扩展到图像序列等更复杂输入
analysis.primary_area_en: neurosymbolic & hybrid AI systems (physics-informed, logic & formal reasoning, etc.)
analysis.category: 符号推理 + 神经网络
analysis.sub_area: 符号推理 + 神经网络
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种可微神经网络架构，从完全可观测但动作参数未观测的状态轨迹中学习提升动作模式。

papers.title: D-VLA: A High-Concurrency Distributed Asynchronous Reinforcement Learning Framework for Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.abstract: The rapid evolution of Embodied AI has enabled Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models to excel in multimodal perception and task execution. However, applying Reinforcement Learning (RL) to these massive models in large-scale distributed environments faces severe systemic bottlenecks, primarily due to the resource conflict between high-fidelity physical simulation and the intensive VRAM/bandwidth demands of deep learning. This conflict often leaves overall throughput constrained by execution-phase inefficiencies. To address these challenges, we propose D-VLA, a high-concurrency, low-latency distributed RL framework for large-scale embodied foundation models. D-VLA introduces "Plane Decoupling," physically isolating high-frequency training data from low-frequency weight control to eliminate interference between simulation and optimization. We further design a four-thread asynchronous "Swimlane" pipeline, enabling full parallel overlap of sampling, inference, gradient computation, and parameter distribution. Additionally, a dual-pool VRAM management model and topology-aware replication resolve memory fragmentation and optimize communication efficiency. Experiments on benchmarks like LIBERO show that D-VLA significantly outperforms mainstream RL frameworks in throughput and sampling efficiency for billion-parameter VLA models. In trillion-parameter scalability tests, our framework maintains exceptional stability and linear speedup, providing a robust system for high-performance general-purpose embodied agents.

analysis.tldr: 提出高并发分布式异步强化学习框架D-VLA，解决VLA模型在分布式RL中的资源冲突与吞吐瓶颈。
analysis.research_motivation: 具身AI中VLA模型规模巨大，分布式RL面临物理仿真与深度学习资源冲突，导致吞吐受限。
analysis.problem: 大规模VLA模型在分布式RL训练中，高保真物理仿真与深度学习VRAM/带宽需求冲突，执行阶段效率低下。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出资源冲突使整体吞吐受限于执行阶段低效，未提供具体失败模式。
analysis.method: 提出D-VLA框架，包含平面解耦（物理隔离高频训练数据与低频权重控制）、四线程异步泳道流水线（采样、推理、梯度计算、参数分发全并行）、双池VRAM管理和拓扑感知复制。
contributions: 提出平面解耦策略消除仿真与优化间的干扰
设计四线程异步泳道流水线实现全并行重叠
提出双池VRAM管理和拓扑感知复制解决内存碎片和通信效率
analysis.experiments: 在LIBERO等基准上，D-VLA在十亿参数VLA模型的吞吐和采样效率上显著优于主流RL框架；万亿参数可扩展性测试中保持稳定和线性加速。
limitations: 摘要未提及对非VLA模型的适用性
可能依赖特定硬件拓扑
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 机器人/自动化/规划
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出高并发分布式异步强化学习框架D-VLA，解决VLA模型在分布式RL中的资源冲突与吞吐瓶颈。

papers.title: Respecting Self-Uncertainty in On-Policy Self-Distillation for Efficient LLM Reasoning
papers.abstract: On-policy self-distillation trains a reasoning model on its own rollouts while a teacher, often the same model conditioned on privileged context, provides dense token-level supervision. Existing objectives typically weight the teacher's token-level signal uniformly across a chain-of-thought sequence, despite substantial variation in the entropy of the teacher's predictive distribution. We propose EGRSD (Entropy-Guided Reinforced Self-Distillation), which unifies token-level updates through three signals: a reward-grounded direction, a teacher-student likelihood-ratio magnitude, and the proposed teacher-entropy confidence gate that down-weights high-entropy token positions while maintaining a nonzero lower bound on every token weight. We further introduce CL-EGRSD, a causal-lookahead variant that distinguishes sustained high-entropy spans from transient high-entropy positions whose following context rapidly becomes low entropy. Experiments with Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B in thinking mode show that EGRSD and CL-EGRSD advance the accuracy-length frontier among the compared trainable methods.

analysis.tldr: 提出熵引导的自蒸馏方法EGRSD，通过动态加权教师信号提升LLM推理效率。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有on-policy自蒸馏方法对教师token级信号均匀加权，忽略了不同token位置预测熵的差异，导致训练效率低下。
analysis.problem: 如何根据教师模型的不确定性动态调整token级蒸馏权重，以提升推理模型在准确率和生成长度之间的权衡。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 教师模型在不同token位置的预测熵存在显著差异，高熵位置通常对应推理中的关键决策点，均匀加权会浪费监督信号。
analysis.method: 提出EGRSD，结合奖励引导方向、师生似然比幅度和教师熵置信门控（对高熵token降权但保持非零下界）；进一步提出CL-EGRSD，区分持续高熵区间和瞬态高熵位置。
contributions: 提出熵引导的置信门控机制，动态调整token级蒸馏权重
引入因果前瞻变体CL-EGRSD，区分高熵的持续与瞬态模式
在Qwen3-4B/8B上验证方法在准确率-长度前沿上的优势
analysis.experiments: 在Qwen3-4B和Qwen3-8B的thinking模式下进行实验，EGRSD和CL-EGRSD在准确率-长度前沿上优于对比的可训练方法。
limitations: 实验仅基于Qwen3系列模型，泛化性待验证
未提供与其他蒸馏方法（如离线蒸馏）的对比
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出熵引导的自蒸馏方法EGRSD，通过动态加权教师信号提升LLM推理效率。

papers.title: It's not the Language Model, it's the Tool: Deterministic Mediation for Scientific Workflows
papers.abstract: Language models can produce convincing scientific analyses, but repeated generations on the same data do not guarantee the same result. A researcher may regenerate an identical query and receive a different fit, a different peak position or a different analysis procedure, without an obvious way to decide which output to trust. We propose typed mediation, a pattern in which the model orchestrates deterministic tools rather than generating analytical code. Each tool encodes one researcher's exact procedure for one instrument, ported through structured interviews. The model selects which tool to call and with what parameters. The tool produces the result. Regeneration does not change it. We evaluate this claim by running the same photoluminescence analysis on four platforms, including three commercial foundation models, four times each with the same prompt. The typed tool produces identical results across all runs. The commercial platforms either vary in numerical output and analytical methodology across runs, or fail to produce valid results on the task. We deploy this pattern on two instruments serving users over approximately six months, with very positive user feedback. Both cases are very challenging: they involve proprietary binary formats and per-seat licensed software, which force the tool to remain on local infrastructure alongside the data and the instrument it operates. We argue that deployment topology is not just a preference, but a structural requirement of scientific tool mediation. The result is a practical pattern for deploying language models in scientific workflows where reproducibility is mandatory, reducing analysis time from weeks to minutes while guaranteeing identical outputs across runs.

analysis.tldr: 提出类型中介模式，让语言模型调用确定性工具而非生成分析代码，确保科学工作流中分析结果的可复现性。
analysis.research_motivation: 语言模型在科学分析中生成结果不稳定，同一查询多次生成可能得到不同输出，研究者难以判断哪个结果可信，威胁科学可复现性。
analysis.problem: 语言模型直接生成分析代码导致结果不可复现，需要一种方法保证相同输入始终产生相同输出。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 商业基础模型在相同提示下多次运行，数值输出和分析方法会变化，甚至无法产生有效结果；而类型化工具在所有运行中产生相同结果。
analysis.method: 类型中介（typed mediation）：模型编排确定性工具而非生成分析代码，每个工具编码一位研究者的精确流程，通过结构化访谈移植，模型选择工具和参数，工具产生结果，重新生成不改变结果。
contributions: 提出类型中介模式，确保科学工作流中语言模型分析结果的可复现性
在四个平台（包括三个商业基础模型）上验证，类型化工具在所有运行中产生相同结果，而商业平台结果变化或失败
在两个真实仪器上部署约六个月，用户反馈非常积极，将分析时间从数周缩短到数分钟
analysis.experiments: 在四个平台上运行相同的光致发光分析，包括三个商业基础模型，每个模型用相同提示运行四次，比较结果一致性。
limitations: 依赖将研究者流程编码为确定性工具，可能不适用于需要探索性分析的任务
工具需要部署在本地基础设施上，可能增加部署复杂度
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出类型中介模式，让语言模型调用确定性工具而非生成分析代码，确保科学工作流中分析结果的可复现性。

papers.title: Improving Code Translation with Syntax-Guided and Semantic-aware Preference Optimization
papers.abstract: LLMs have shown immense potential for code translation, yet they often struggle to ensure both syntactic correctness and semantic consistency. While preference-based learning offers a promising alignment strategy, it is hindered by unreliable semantic rewards derived from sparse test cases or restrictive reference translations. We argue that a robust semantic reward for code translation must be derived directly from the source code. In this paper, we propose CTO to improve code translation with syntax-guided and semantic-aware preference optimization. Through contrastive learning, we train a cross-lingual semantic model to directly assess functional equivalence between source and translated code. By formulating code translation as a multi-objective optimization problem, this robust semantic signal is seamlessly unified with compiler-based syntactic feedback within the direct preference optimization framework. Extensive experiments on C++, Java, and Python translations demonstrate that CTO significantly outperforms existing baselines and alternative preference optimization strategies.

analysis.tldr: 提出CTO方法，通过语法引导和语义感知偏好优化提升代码翻译的语法正确性与语义一致性。
analysis.research_motivation: LLM在代码翻译中难以同时保证语法正确性和语义一致性，现有偏好学习方法依赖稀疏测试用例或限制性参考翻译，语义奖励不可靠。
analysis.problem: 如何为代码翻译构建鲁棒的语义奖励，并与语法反馈统一优化，以提升翻译质量。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 通过对比学习训练跨语言语义模型直接评估源与翻译代码的功能等价性，将代码翻译建模为多目标优化问题，在直接偏好优化框架中统一鲁棒语义信号与编译器语法反馈。
contributions: 提出CTO方法，结合语法引导和语义感知偏好优化提升代码翻译。
通过对比学习训练跨语言语义模型，直接评估功能等价性。
将代码翻译作为多目标优化问题，统一语义信号与语法反馈。
analysis.experiments: 在C++、Java和Python翻译上，CTO显著优于现有基线和替代偏好优化策略。
limitations: 可能依赖编译器反馈，对无编译器的语言适用性有限。
对比学习训练可能需要大量平行语料。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出CTO方法，通过语法引导和语义感知偏好优化提升代码翻译的语法正确性与语义一致性。

papers.title: An Agentic AI Framework with Large Language Models and Chain-of-Thought for UAV-Assisted Logistics Scheduling with Mobile Edge Computing
papers.abstract: In cloud manufacturing, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) can support both product collection and mobile edge computing (MEC). This joint operation forms a hybrid scheduling problem, where physical logistics decisions are coupled with computational task scheduling. In this paper, UAVs collect finished products from manufacturing stations and transport them back to a central depot. Meanwhile, computational tasks generated by industrial sensor devices at these stations are processed locally, at UAVs, or offloaded via UAVs to the cloud. This coupling makes the problem challenging. A UAV can provide MEC services only during its service window at a station, so routing decisions directly determine when UAV-assisted offloading is available. Routing decisions also affect the UAV energy budget and the availability of onboard computing and communication resources for computational task execution under task deadline constraints. To address this, we propose an agentic-AI-assisted optimization framework with two components. First, we develop an agentic AI that combines large language models, retrieval-augmented generation, and chain-of-thought reasoning to translate user input into an interpretable mathematical formulation for the hybrid scheduling problem. Second, we design a hierarchical deep reinforcement learning approach based on proximal policy optimization (PPO), where the upper layer learns UAV routing and the lower layer optimizes per-slot task execution and resource allocation. Simulation results show that the proposed framework yields more consistent formulations, while the hierarchical PPO achieves full product collection in 99.6% of the last 500 episodes and maintains a 100% deadline satisfaction rate, with more stable performance than the advantage actor-critic approach.

analysis.tldr: 提出一个结合LLM和层次化强化学习的智能体框架，用于无人机辅助物流与边缘计算联合调度。
analysis.research_motivation: 无人机同时承担产品收集和移动边缘计算任务，形成物流与计算调度的耦合问题，传统方法难以处理这种复杂约束。
analysis.problem: 解决无人机物流路线与计算任务卸载的联合调度优化问题。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 无人机路由决策直接影响MEC服务窗口、能量预算和计算资源可用性，导致问题高度耦合。
analysis.method: 提出智能体AI框架：上层用LLM+RAG+CoT将用户输入转化为数学公式，下层用层次化PPO分别优化路由和任务执行。
contributions: 提出结合LLM和层次化强化学习的智能体框架解决联合调度问题
层次化PPO方法在仿真中实现99.6%产品收集率和100%截止时间满足率
analysis.experiments: 仿真实验显示层次化PPO在最后500个episode中实现99.6%产品收集率和100%截止时间满足率，性能优于优势演员-评论家方法。
limitations: 仅仿真验证，未提及真实环境部署
LLM生成数学公式的泛化性未充分讨论
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 机器人/自动化/规划
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一个结合LLM和层次化强化学习的智能体框架，用于无人机辅助物流与边缘计算联合调度。

papers.title: Hierarchical Attacks for Multi-Modal Multi-Agent Reasoning
papers.abstract: Multi-modal multi-agent systems (MM-MAS) have gained increasing attention for their capacity to enable complex reasoning and coordination across diverse modalities. As these systems continue to expand in scale and functionality, investigating their potential vulnerabilities has become increasingly important. However, existing studies on adversarial attacks in multi-agent systems primarily focus on isolated agents or unimodal settings, leaving the vulnerabilities of MM-MAS largely underexplored. To bridge this gap, we introduce HAM$^{3}$, a Hierarchical Attack framework for multi-modal multi-agent systems that decomposes attacks into three interconnected layers. Specifically, at the perception layer, HAM$^{3}$ mounts attacks by perturbing visual inputs, textual inputs, and their fused visual-textual representations. At the communication layer, it performs communication-level attacks that corrupt message content and interaction topology, such as manipulating shared context or communication links to distort collective information flow. At the reasoning layer, it conducts reasoning-level attacks that interfere with each agent's cognitive pipeline, biasing reasoning trajectories and ultimately compromising final decisions. We evaluate HAM$^{3}$ on the GQA benchmark through multi-agent systems built on distinct reasoning paradigms including ReAct, Plan-and-Solve, and Reflexion. Experiments demonstrate that our framework achieves an Attack Success Rate of up to 78.3%, with reasoning-layer attacks being the most effective. More than half of the successful attacks lead multiple agents to produce consistent errors. These findings offer valuable insights for building more robust and interpretable multi-agent intelligence.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种分层攻击框架HAM³，针对多模态多智能体系统的感知、通信和推理层进行攻击，在GQA基准上达到78.3%攻击成功率。
analysis.research_motivation: 多模态多智能体系统（MM-MAS）在复杂推理和协调中应用广泛，但其脆弱性研究不足，现有攻击主要针对孤立智能体或单模态场景，缺乏对MM-MAS系统级漏洞的探索。
analysis.problem: 如何系统性地攻击多模态多智能体系统，揭示其在感知、通信和推理层面的脆弱性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 推理层攻击效果最强，超过一半的成功攻击导致多个智能体产生一致错误。
analysis.method: 提出HAM³分层攻击框架，包含三个层次：感知层攻击（扰动视觉、文本及融合表示）、通信层攻击（破坏消息内容和交互拓扑）、推理层攻击（干扰认知管道，偏置推理轨迹）。
contributions: 首次提出针对多模态多智能体系统的分层攻击框架HAM³，覆盖感知、通信和推理三个层面。
在GQA基准上通过多种推理范式（ReAct、Plan-and-Solve、Reflexion）验证了攻击有效性，最高攻击成功率达78.3%。
analysis.experiments: 在GQA基准上使用基于ReAct、Plan-and-Solve和Reflexion推理范式的多智能体系统进行评估，实验表明框架达到78.3%的攻击成功率，且推理层攻击最有效。
limitations: 仅在一个基准（GQA）上评估，泛化性有待验证。
未讨论防御机制或缓解策略。
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种分层攻击框架HAM³，针对多模态多智能体系统的感知、通信和推理层进行攻击，在GQA基准上达到78.3%攻击成功率。

papers.title: Formal Conjectures: An Open and Evolving Benchmark for Verified Discovery in Mathematics
papers.abstract: As automated reasoning systems advance rapidly, there is a growing need for research-level formal mathematical problems to accurately evaluate their capabilities. To address this, we present Formal Conjectures, an evolving benchmark of currently 2615 mathematical problem statements formalized in Lean 4. Sourced from areas of active mathematical research, the dataset features 1029 open research conjectures providing a zero-contamination benchmark for mathematical proof discovery, and 836 solved problems for proof autoformalization. Notably, the repository provides a structured interface connecting mathematicians who formalize and clarify problems with the AI systems and humans attempting to solve them. Demonstrating its immediate utility, the benchmark has already been leveraged to make new mathematical discoveries, including the resolution of open research conjectures. We describe our approach to ensuring the correctness of these formalizations in a collaborative open-source project where contributions stem from an active community. In this framework, AI-generated proofs and disproofs serve as a valuable auditing mechanism to iteratively improve the fidelity of the benchmark. Finally, we provide a standardized evaluation setup and report baseline results on frozen evaluation subsets, demonstrating a climbable signal that measures the current frontier of automated reasoning on research-level mathematics.

analysis.tldr: 一个包含2615个Lean 4形式化数学问题的开放基准，用于评估自动推理系统，已有AI解决开放猜想。
analysis.research_motivation: 自动推理系统快速发展，但缺乏研究级形式化数学问题来准确评估其能力。
analysis.problem: 构建一个开放、可演进的基准，包含研究级形式化数学问题，用于评估自动推理和证明发现能力。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 从活跃数学研究领域收集问题，用Lean 4形式化，包含开放猜想和已解决问题，提供结构化接口连接数学家和AI系统，并通过社区贡献和AI生成证明/反证来确保正确性。
contributions: 提出了一个包含2615个形式化数学问题的开放基准，其中1029个为开放研究猜想，836个为已解决问题。
提供了标准化评估设置和基线结果，展示了自动推理在数学研究中的当前前沿。
analysis.experiments: 在冻结评估子集上报告了基线结果，显示可攀登的信号。
limitations: 基准规模可能仍有限，覆盖的数学领域不够全面。
形式化过程依赖社区贡献，可能存在不一致性。
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 一个包含2615个Lean 4形式化数学问题的开放基准，用于评估自动推理系统，已有AI解决开放猜想。

papers.title: Strikingness-Aware Evaluation for Temporal Knowledge Graph Reasoning
papers.abstract: Temporal Knowledge Graph Reasoning (TKGR) aims at inferring missing (especially future) events from historical data. Current evaluation in TKGR uniformly weights all events, ignoring that most are trivial repetitions, which overestimate the true reasoning ability. Therefore, the rare outstanding events, whose prediction demands deeper reasoning, should be distinguished and emphasized. To this end, we propose a strikingness-aware evaluation framework, which introduces a rule-based strikingness measuring framework (RSMF) to quantify event strikingness by comparing its expected occurrence with peer events derived from temporal rules. Strikingness is then integrated as a weighting factor into metrics like weighted MRR and Hits@k. Experiments on four TKG benchmarks reveal: 1) All representative models perform worse as event strikingness increases, 2) Path-based methods excel on low-strikingness events and representation-based ones on high-strikingness events, 3) We design an ensemble method whose gains stem from fitting trivial events rather than reasoning improvement. Our framework provides a more rigorous evaluation, refocusing the field on predicting outstanding events.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种基于显著性的时序知识图谱推理评估框架，通过加权指标更严格地衡量模型对罕见重要事件的推理能力。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有TKGR评估对所有事件统一加权，忽略了大多数事件是琐碎重复的，导致高估模型的真实推理能力，因此需要区分并强调那些需要更深层推理的罕见重要事件。
analysis.problem: 如何量化事件的显著性并将其融入评估指标，以更准确地反映模型对非平凡事件的推理能力。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 实验发现：1）所有代表性模型在事件显著性增加时表现更差；2）基于路径的方法在低显著性事件上表现更好，而基于表示的方法在高显著性事件上表现更好；3）设计的集成方法的收益来自拟合琐碎事件而非推理改进。
analysis.method: 提出基于规则的显著性度量框架RSMF，通过比较事件预期发生与从时序规则推导的同类事件来量化显著性，并将显著性作为权重因子集成到加权MRR和Hits@k等指标中。
contributions: 提出显著性感知评估框架，通过加权指标更严格地评估TKGR模型对罕见重要事件的推理能力。
设计基于规则的显著性度量框架RSMF，量化事件显著性。
在四个基准上揭示模型在不同显著性事件上的表现差异，并指出集成方法的局限性。
analysis.experiments: 在四个TKG基准上进行了实验，评估了多个代表性模型，并分析了显著性对性能的影响。
limitations: RSMF依赖于预定义的时序规则，可能无法覆盖所有事件类型。
显著性加权指标可能对噪声敏感，需要进一步验证鲁棒性。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种基于显著性的时序知识图谱推理评估框架，通过加权指标更严格地衡量模型对罕见重要事件的推理能力。

papers.title: A Constraint Programming Approach for $n$-Day Lookahead Playoff Clinching
papers.abstract: In professional sports, a team has clinched the playoffs if they are guaranteed a postseason spot, regardless of the outcomes of any remaining games. As the season progresses, sports fans and other stakeholders are interested in precisely when, and under what conditions, their team will clinch the playoffs. In this paper, we investigate playoff clinching in the context of the National Hockey League (NHL), where it is computationally challenging to produce clinching scenarios due, in part, to complex tie-breakers. We present an algorithm that determines under which combinations of game outcomes in the next $n$ days a team will clinch the playoffs (i.e., "$n$-day lookahead clinching"). Our approach is a custom tree search which employs various preprocessing techniques, pruning strategies, and node ordering heuristics to efficiently explore the space of possible outcomes. The tree search leverages a constraint programming (CP)-based subroutine for inference that determines if a team has clinched the playoffs for some snapshot in time of the regular season (i.e., "0-day lookahead clinching"). This CP subroutine aims to find a counter-example in which the team being evaluated is eliminated, taking into account qualification rules and the NHL's extensive list of tie-breakers. We validate the efficacy of our algorithm using hundreds of scenarios based on public NHL data for the seasons 2021-22 through 2024-25. The methods introduced can be readily extended to other metrics of interest, including mathematical proof of playoff elimination, clinching the President's Trophy, as well as clinching (or being eliminated from clinching) any other seed in the standings.

analysis.tldr: 提出约束规划方法实现NHL季后赛n天前瞻锁定判定。
analysis.research_motivation: 体育赛事中球迷和利益相关者关注球队何时锁定季后赛，但NHL复杂的平局规则使得计算锁定场景具有挑战性。
analysis.problem: 在NHL中，给定未来n天内比赛结果组合，判定球队是否锁定季后赛。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 自定义树搜索，结合预处理、剪枝策略和节点排序启发式，利用约束规划子程序进行0天前瞻锁定判定（寻找反例证明球队被淘汰）。
contributions: 提出n天前瞻锁定季后赛的算法，可处理NHL复杂平局规则。
验证算法在2021-22至2024-25赛季数百个场景下的有效性。
analysis.experiments: 使用2021-22至2024-25赛季的NHL公开数据，基于数百个场景验证算法有效性。
limitations: 仅针对NHL，可能需调整规则才能推广到其他联赛。
未讨论算法在大规模n或完整赛季下的计算效率。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出约束规划方法实现NHL季后赛n天前瞻锁定判定。

papers.title: GRACE: Gradient-aligned Reasoning Data Curation for Efficient Post-training
papers.abstract: Existing reasoning data curation pipelines score whole samples, treating every intermediate step as equally valuable. In reality, steps within a trace contribute very unevenly, and selecting reasoning data well requires assessing them individually. We present GRACE, a gradient-aligned curation method that views each reasoning trace as a sequence of optimization events and scores every step by two complementary signals: its alignment with the answer-oriented gradient direction, and its consistency with the preceding reasoning trajectory. Step-level scores are aggregated into a sample-level value for subset selection, using only the model's internal optimization signals and no external reward models or step annotations. To make this scalable, GRACE introduces a representation-level gradient proxy that estimates step-level alignment from token-level upstream signals in a single forward pass. Post-training Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct on MMathCoT-1M, GRACE reaches 108.8% of the full-data performance with 20% of the data and retains 100.2% with only 5%, with subsets that transfer effectively across model backbones.

analysis.tldr: GRACE 通过梯度对齐的步骤级评分，仅用 5% 数据即可达到全量数据性能，实现高效推理数据筛选。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有推理数据筛选方法对整条轨迹统一评分，忽略了中间步骤的价值差异，导致数据利用效率低。
analysis.problem: 如何根据步骤对最终答案的贡献差异，从推理轨迹中高效筛选出最有价值的子集。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 推理轨迹中不同步骤对最终答案的贡献极不均匀，需要步骤级评估。
analysis.method: 提出 GRACE，将每条推理轨迹视为优化事件序列，利用与答案梯度方向的对齐度和与前序轨迹的一致性两个信号对每个步骤评分，再聚合为样本级分数进行子集选择；通过表示级梯度代理实现单次前向传播的步骤级对齐估计。
contributions: 提出步骤级梯度对齐评分方法，无需外部奖励模型或步骤标注。
引入表示级梯度代理，实现可扩展的步骤级对齐估计。
实验证明仅用 5% 数据即可达到全量数据性能，且子集可跨模型迁移。
analysis.experiments: 在 Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct 上使用 MMathCoT-1M 进行后训练，20% 数据达到全量性能的 108.8%，5% 数据保留 100.2% 性能，且子集可跨骨干模型迁移。
limitations: 方法依赖模型内部梯度信号，可能受梯度噪声影响。
仅验证了视觉-语言模型，未在纯文本或更大规模模型上测试。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: GRACE 通过梯度对齐的步骤级评分，仅用 5% 数据即可达到全量数据性能，实现高效推理数据筛选。

papers.title: An Agentic LLM-Based Framework for Population-Scale Mental Health Screening
papers.abstract: Mental health disorders affect millions worldwide, and healthcare systems are increasingly overwhelmed by the volume of clinical data generated from electronic records, telemedicine platforms, and population-level screening programs. At the same time, the emergence of novel AI-based approaches in healthcare calls for intelligent frameworks capable of processing domain-specific unstructured clinical information while adapting to patient-specific needs. This paper proposes an agentic framework for building robust LLM-based pipelines, where each stage is encapsulated as a LangChain agent governed by explicit policies and proxy-guided evaluation. Stages are incrementally locked once validated, ensuring that later adaptations cannot overwrite configurations without demonstrated improvement. The proposed framework evolves from feature-level exploration, through proxy-based tuning and freeze/rollback mechanisms, to full orchestration by an Orchestrator Agent that coordinates preprocessing, retrieval, selection, diversity, threshold optimization, and decoding. A proof-of-concept in transcript-based depression detection demonstrates that the framework converges to stable configurations, such as cosine similarity, dynamic Top-k, and threshold 0.75, while controlling evaluation costs and avoiding regressions. These results highlight the potential of agentic AI to enable population-level mental health screening over large clinical datasets, addressing critical challenges in trustworthiness, reproducibility, and adaptability required in healthcare environments.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种基于LLM的智能体框架，用于大规模心理健康筛查，通过分阶段锁定和代理引导评估实现稳定配置。
analysis.research_motivation: 心理健康障碍影响全球数百万人，医疗系统被临床数据淹没，现有AI方法缺乏处理非结构化临床信息和适应患者特定需求的智能框架。
analysis.problem: 如何构建一个稳健、可复现且适应性强的LLM管道，用于大规模心理健康筛查中的非结构化临床数据处理。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出一个智能体框架，每个阶段封装为LangChain代理，由显式策略和代理引导评估控制，阶段验证后锁定，通过Orchestrator Agent协调预处理、检索、选择、多样性、阈值优化和解码。
contributions: 提出一个基于LLM的智能体框架，用于构建稳健的医疗筛查管道。
通过分阶段锁定和回滚机制确保配置稳定性和可复现性。
在基于转录的抑郁症检测中验证框架收敛到稳定配置（余弦相似度、动态Top-k、阈值0.75），控制评估成本并避免回归。
analysis.experiments: 在基于转录的抑郁症检测概念验证中，框架收敛到稳定配置，如余弦相似度、动态Top-k和阈值0.75，同时控制评估成本并避免回归。
limitations: 仅在一个概念验证任务（抑郁症检测）上测试，泛化性未知。
未提及与其他基线方法的定量比较。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种基于LLM的智能体框架，用于大规模心理健康筛查，通过分阶段锁定和代理引导评估实现稳定配置。

papers.title: MAP: A Map-then-Act Paradigm for Long-Horizon Interactive Agent Reasoning
papers.abstract: Current interactive LLM agents rely on goal-conditioned stepwise planning, where environmental understanding is acquired reactively during execution rather than established beforehand. This temporal inversion leads to Delayed Environmental Perception: agents must infer environmental constraints through trial-and-error, resulting in an Epistemic Bottleneck that traps them in inefficient failure cycles. Inspired by human affordance perception and cognitive map theory, we propose the Map-then-Act Paradigm (MAP), a plug-and-play framework that shifts environment understanding before execution. MAP consists of three stages: (1) Global Exploration, acquiring environment-general priors; (2) Task-Specific Mapping, constructing a structured cognitive map; and (3) Knowledge-Augmented Execution, solving tasks grounded on the map. Experiments show consistent gains across benchmarks and LLMs. On ARC-AGI-3, MAP enables frontier models to surpass near-zero baseline performance in 22 of 25 game environments. We further introduce MAP-2K, a dataset of map-then-act trajectories, and show that training on it outperforms expert execution traces, suggesting that understanding environments is more fundamental than imitation.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种先构建认知地图再执行任务的范式，显著提升LLM在长时交互推理中的表现。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前交互式LLM代理依赖目标条件逐步规划，环境理解在执行过程中被动获取，导致延迟环境感知和认知瓶颈，陷入低效失败循环。
analysis.problem: 解决LLM代理在长时交互任务中因缺乏预先环境理解而导致的试错效率低下问题。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出当前方法存在Delayed Environmental Perception和Epistemic Bottleneck，即代理必须通过试错推断环境约束，导致失败循环。
analysis.method: 提出Map-then-Act Paradigm (MAP)，包含三个阶段：全局探索获取环境通用先验、任务特定映射构建结构化认知地图、知识增强执行基于地图求解任务。
contributions: 提出MAP框架，将环境理解前置，作为即插即用模块提升LLM代理性能。
引入MAP-2K数据集，证明基于地图-行动轨迹的训练优于专家执行轨迹。
analysis.experiments: 在ARC-AGI-3基准上，MAP使前沿模型在25个游戏环境中的22个上超越接近零的基线性能。
limitations: 可能依赖高质量的环境探索阶段，在复杂动态环境中探索成本高。
认知地图的构建和更新机制可能无法完全适应环境变化。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 机器人/自动化/规划
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种先构建认知地图再执行任务的范式，显著提升LLM在长时交互推理中的表现。

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Tutoring for Algorithm Tracing and Problem-Solving in AI Education
papers.abstract: Students learning algorithms often need support as they interpret traces, debug reasoning errors, and apply procedures across unfamiliar problem instances. In this paper, we present KITE (Knowledge-Informed Tutoring Engine), a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)-based intelligent tutoring system designed to serve as a classroom teaching assistant for algorithmic reasoning and problem-solving tasks. KITE uses an intent-aware Socratic response strategy to tailor support to different student needs, responding with targeted hints, guiding questions, and progressive scaffolding intended to strengthen students' algorithmic problem-solving ability. To keep responses aligned with course content, KITE uses a multimodal RAG pipeline that retrieves relevant information from course materials. We evaluate KITE using three forms of assessment: RAGAs-based metrics for response grounding and quality, expert evaluation of pedagogical quality, and a simulated student pipeline in which a weaker language model interacts with KITE across two-turn dialogues and produces revised answers after receiving feedback. Results indicate that KITE produces contextually grounded and pedagogically appropriate responses. Further, using simulated students, KITE's feedback helped the student models produce more accurate follow-up responses on procedural and tracing questions, suggesting that its scaffolding can support algorithmic problem-solving. This work contributes a tutoring architecture and an evaluation approach for assessing retrieval-grounded explanations and scaffolded problem-solving feedback.

analysis.tldr: 提出基于RAG的智能辅导系统KITE，通过意图感知的苏格拉底式策略和课程材料检索，提升学生算法追踪与问题解决能力。
analysis.research_motivation: 学生在学习算法时，需要解释追踪、调试推理错误并在陌生问题中应用过程，但现有辅导系统缺乏针对性的、与课程内容对齐的支架式支持。
analysis.problem: 如何构建一个能根据学生不同需求提供上下文相关、课程对齐的算法推理辅导的智能系统。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出KITE系统，采用意图感知的苏格拉底式响应策略，结合多模态RAG管道从课程材料中检索相关信息，生成针对性提示、引导问题和渐进式支架。
contributions: 提出KITE，一个基于RAG的智能辅导系统，用于算法追踪和问题解决。
设计意图感知的苏格拉底式响应策略，根据学生需求提供差异化支持。
提出包含RAGAs指标、专家评估和模拟学生管道的多维度评估方法。
analysis.experiments: 使用RAGAs指标评估响应基础性和质量，专家评估教学法质量，以及模拟学生管道（弱语言模型与KITE两轮对话后修正答案）评估反馈效果。结果表明KITE产生上下文相关且教学法适当的响应，模拟学生模型在程序和追踪问题上答案更准确。
limitations: 评估基于模拟学生，真实课堂效果未知。
可能依赖课程材料质量，对未覆盖内容效果有限。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出基于RAG的智能辅导系统KITE，通过意图感知的苏格拉底式策略和课程材料检索，提升学生算法追踪与问题解决能力。

papers.title: Useful Memories Become Faulty When Continuously Updated by LLMs
papers.abstract: Learning from past experience benefits from two complementary forms of memory: episodic traces -- raw trajectories of what happened -- and consolidated abstractions distilled across many episodes into reusable, schema-like lessons. Recent agentic-memory systems pursue the consolidated form: an LLM rewrites past trajectories into a textual memory bank that it continuously updates with new interactions, promising self-improving agents without parameter updates. Yet we find that such consolidated memories produced by today's LLMs are often faulty even when derived from useful experiences. As consolidation proceeds, memory utility first rises, then degrades, and can fall below the no-memory baseline. More surprisingly, even when consolidating from ground-truth solutions, GPT-5.4 fails on 54% of a set of ARC-AGI problems it had previously solved without memory. We trace the regression to the consolidation step rather than the underlying experience: the same trajectories yield qualitatively different memories under different update schedules, and an episodic-only control that simply retains those trajectories remains competitive with the consolidators we test. In a controlled ARC-AGI Stream environment that exposes Retain, Delete, and Consolidate actions, agents preserve raw episodes by default and double the accuracy of their forced-consolidation counterparts; disabling consolidation entirely (episodic management only) matches this auto regime. Practically, robust agent memory should treat raw episodes as first-class evidence and gate consolidation explicitly rather than firing it after every interaction. Looking forward, reliable agentic memory will require LLMs that can consolidate without overwriting the evidence they depend on.

analysis.tldr: LLM持续更新记忆反而导致性能下降，应保留原始轨迹而非过度整合。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有智能体记忆系统依赖LLM将经验整合为文本记忆库以实现持续改进，但整合过程可能引入错误，影响记忆效用。
analysis.problem: LLM在持续更新整合记忆时，记忆效用先升后降，甚至低于无记忆基线，且整合过程会破坏原始经验。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要观察到：整合记忆效用随更新先升后降；GPT-5.4在ARC-AGI问题上使用整合记忆后失败率高达54%；不同更新调度导致不同记忆质量；仅保留原始轨迹的对照方法表现与整合方法相当。
analysis.method: 提出ARC-AGI Stream环境，包含Retain、Delete、Consolidate动作，默认保留原始轨迹，仅在必要时显式整合。
contributions: 发现LLM持续更新整合记忆会导致性能退化，甚至低于无记忆基线。
提出应优先保留原始轨迹，显式控制整合而非每次交互后自动整合。
analysis.experiments: 在ARC-AGI问题上进行实验，比较整合记忆、仅保留原始轨迹和无记忆基线，结果显示仅保留原始轨迹的准确率是强制整合的两倍。
limitations: 实验仅基于ARC-AGI问题，可能不适用于其他任务。
未提供具体模型和数据集细节。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: LLM持续更新记忆反而导致性能下降，应保留原始轨迹而非过度整合。

papers.title: Retrieval is Cheap, Show Me the Code: Executable Multi-Hop Reasoning for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
papers.abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become a standard approach for knowledge-intensive question answering, but existing systems remain brittle on multi-hop questions, where solving the task requires chaining multiple retrieval and reasoning steps. Key challenges are that current methods represent reasoning through free-form natural language, where intermediate states are implicit, retrieval queries can drift from intended entities, and errors are detected by the same model that produces them making self-reflection an unreliable, ungrounded signal. We observe that multi-hop question answering is a typical form of step-by-step computation, and that this structured process aligns closely with how code-specialized language models are trained to operate. Motivated by this, we introduce \pyrag, a framework that reformulates multi-hop RAG as program synthesis and execution. Instead of free-form reasoning trajectories, \pyrag represents the reasoning process as an executable Python program over retrieval and QA tools, exposing intermediate states as variables, producing deterministic feedback through execution, and yielding an inspectable trace of the entire reasoning process. This formulation further enables compiler-grounded self-repair and execution-driven adaptive retrieval without any additional training. Experiments on five QA benchmarks (PopQA, HotpotQA, 2WikiMultihopQA, MuSiQue, and Bamboogle) show that \pyrag consistently outperforms strong baselines under both training-free and RL-trained settings, with especially large gains on compositional multi-hop datasets. Our code, data and models are publicly available at this https URL .

analysis.tldr: 将多跳RAG转化为可执行Python程序，通过代码执行实现确定性反馈和自修复，显著提升多跳问答性能。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有RAG系统在多跳问答中表现脆弱，因为自由形式的自然语言推理导致中间状态隐式、检索查询易漂移，且错误检测依赖同一模型，自我反思不可靠。
analysis.problem: 多跳问答中检索与推理步骤的链式组合缺乏结构化、可验证的中间表示，导致错误累积和不可靠的自我修正。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 多跳问答本质上是逐步计算过程，与代码专用语言模型的训练方式高度契合；自由形式推理的中间状态隐式且错误检测不可靠。
analysis.method: 提出PyRAG框架，将多跳RAG重写为程序合成与执行：用可执行Python程序表示推理过程，通过检索和QA工具调用，将中间状态暴露为变量，利用执行产生确定性反馈，并支持编译器引导的自修复和执行驱动的自适应检索。
contributions: 提出将多跳RAG转化为程序合成与执行的新范式，利用代码执行提供确定性反馈和可检查轨迹。
实现编译器引导的自修复和执行驱动的自适应检索，无需额外训练。
在五个QA基准上取得一致优势，尤其在组合多跳数据集上提升显著。
analysis.experiments: 在PopQA、HotpotQA、2WikiMultihopQA、MuSiQue和Bamboogle五个基准上评估，在无训练和RL训练设置下均优于强基线。
limitations: 依赖代码生成模型的质量，可能对复杂逻辑推理仍有挑战。
执行环境的安全性和资源消耗需进一步考虑。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 将多跳RAG转化为可执行Python程序，通过代码执行实现确定性反馈和自修复，显著提升多跳问答性能。

papers.title: Position: Agentic AI System Is a Foreseeable Pathway to AGI
papers.abstract: Is monolithic scaling the only path to AGI? This paper challenges the dogma that purely scaling a single model is sufficient to achieve Artificial General Intelligence. Instead, we identify Agentic AI as a necessary paradigm for mastering the complex, heterogeneous distribution of real-world tasks. Through rigorous theoretical derivations, we contrast the optimization constraints of monolithic learners against the efficiency of Agentic systems, progressing from simple routing mechanisms to general Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) topologies. We demonstrate that Agentic AI achieves exponentially superior generalization and sample efficiency. Finally, we discuss the connection to Mixture-of-Experts, reinterpret the instability of current multi-agent frameworks, and call for greater research focus on Agentic AI.

analysis.tldr: 论文论证单体缩放不是AGI的唯一路径，提出Agentic AI系统通过DAG拓扑实现指数级泛化优势。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前主流观点认为单纯缩放单一模型即可实现AGI，但现实任务分布复杂异构，单体模型存在优化约束和效率瓶颈，亟需探索新范式。
analysis.problem: 证明单体缩放不足以实现AGI，并论证Agentic AI系统作为替代路径的理论优势。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体实验现象，但理论推导表明Agentic AI在泛化性和样本效率上具有指数级优势，并重新解释了当前多智能体框架的不稳定性。
analysis.method: 通过理论推导对比单体学习器与Agentic系统的优化约束，从简单路由机制推广到一般有向无环图（DAG）拓扑，并讨论与混合专家模型（MoE）的联系。
contributions: 挑战了单体缩放是实现AGI唯一路径的主流观点，提出Agentic AI是必要范式。
从理论上证明Agentic AI系统在泛化性和样本效率上具有指数级优势。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 理论推导缺乏实证验证，未在具体任务上展示性能。
未详细说明DAG拓扑的设计原则和可扩展性。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 论文论证单体缩放不是AGI的唯一路径，提出Agentic AI系统通过DAG拓扑实现指数级泛化优势。

papers.title: Sustaining AI safety: Control-theoretic external impossibility, intrinsic necessity, and structural requirements
papers.abstract: As AI systems become increasingly capable, safety strategies must be evaluated not only by how much they reduce present risk, but by whether they could sustain safety once external control can no longer reliably constrain system behavior. This paper addresses that problem by using control theory to clarify, at a structural level, whether externally enforced safety-sustaining strategies can succeed and, if not, what any alternative strategy would have to satisfy in order to be viable. It establishes two main results. First, under explicit premises including a reachability condition, it proves a class-wide external impossibility result: once the system's effects exceed what bounded external control can counteract, no strategy that depends in any degree on continued external enforcement can sustain AI safety. This failure is structural across the entire externally enforced class rather than contingent on any particular strategy. Second, it establishes a conditional class-level necessity result: if at least one candidate safety-sustaining strategy remains after that elimination, then all such remaining strategies must be intrinsic. It then states four structural requirements for viability: safety may not depend on continued external enforcement; the system's terminal objective must be safety-compatible when first formed; that objective must remain stable under self-modification; and safety must continue to be preserved as capability grows. The paper does not propose a complete strategy for sustaining AI safety. Its contribution is to give formal structure to a widely held concern about the limits of external control. It does so by deriving explicit conditional results that identify which safety-sustaining strategies are ruled out and what any remaining strategies must satisfy.

analysis.tldr: 用控制论证明外部控制无法持续保障AI安全，并推导出内在安全策略必须满足的结构性要求。
analysis.research_motivation: 随着AI能力增强，外部控制可能无法可靠约束系统行为，现有安全策略缺乏对长期可持续性的结构性分析。
analysis.problem: 从控制论角度分析外部强制安全策略的可行性，并确定替代策略必须满足的条件。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 基于控制理论，建立可达性条件等前提，证明外部强制策略的类级不可能性，并推导内在策略的必要性及四个结构要求。
contributions: 证明了外部强制安全策略在结构上不可能持续保障AI安全。
推导出任何剩余可行策略必须满足的四个结构性要求。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 未提出完整的可持续安全策略。
结果依赖于明确的前提假设，实际应用可能受限。
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 用控制论证明外部控制无法持续保障AI安全，并推导出内在安全策略必须满足的结构性要求。

papers.title: When Attention Closes: How LLMs Lose the Thread in Multi-Turn Interaction
papers.abstract: Large language models can follow complex instructions in a single turn, yet over long multi-turn interactions they often lose the thread of instructions, persona, and rules. This degradation has been measured behaviorally but not mechanistically explained. We propose a channel-transition account: goal-defining tokens become less accessible through attention, while goal-related information may persist in residual representations. We introduce the Goal Accessibility Ratio (GAR), measuring attention from generated tokens to task-defining goal tokens, and combine it with sliding-window ablations and residual-stream probes. When attention to instructions closes, what survives reveals architecture. Across architectures, the transition yields qualitatively distinct failure modes: some models preserve goal-conditioned behavior at vanishing attention, others fail despite decodable residual goal information, and the layer at which this encoding emerges varies from 2 to 27. A within-model causal ablation that force-closes the attention channel in Mistral collapses recall from near-perfect to 11% on a 20-fact retention task and raises persona-constraint violations above an adversarial-pressure baseline without user pressure, with both effects emerging at the predictable crossover turn. Linear probes recover per-episode recall outcomes from residual representations with AUC up to 0.99 across all four primary architectures, while input embeddings remain at chance. Across architectures and model scales, the gap between attention loss and residual decodability predicts whether goal-conditioned behavior survives channel closure. We contribute GAR as a diagnostic, the channel-transition framework as a controlled mechanistic account, and a parametric prediction of failure timing under windowed attention closure.

analysis.tldr: 提出注意力通道关闭假说，解释LLM在多轮交互中丢失指令线索的机制，并引入目标可访问率（GAR）作为诊断工具。
analysis.research_motivation: 大语言模型在单轮交互中能遵循复杂指令，但在长多轮交互中常丢失指令、角色和规则线索，已有行为测量但缺乏机制解释。
analysis.problem: 解释LLM在多轮交互中丢失指令线索的机制，并量化注意力通道关闭对目标保持行为的影响。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 目标定义token通过注意力变得不可访问，而目标相关信息可能残留在残差表示中；不同架构下注意力关闭导致定性不同的失败模式。
analysis.method: 提出目标可访问率（GAR）度量从生成token到任务定义目标token的注意力，结合滑动窗口消融和残差流探针分析注意力通道关闭的影响。
contributions: 提出通道转换框架作为多轮交互中指令丢失的受控机制解释
引入GAR作为诊断注意力关闭的诊断工具
跨架构和模型规模预测注意力窗口关闭下的失败时机
analysis.experiments: 在Mistral模型上进行因果消融，强制关闭注意力通道使20事实保持任务从近乎完美降至11%，角色约束违反超过对抗压力基线；线性探针从残差表示恢复每轮召回结果，AUC高达0.99。
limitations: 主要分析基于有限架构和任务，可能不覆盖所有LLM类型
GAR诊断的有效性可能依赖于特定任务设置
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出注意力通道关闭假说，解释LLM在多轮交互中丢失指令线索的机制，并引入目标可访问率（GAR）作为诊断工具。

papers.title: Beyond Cooperative Simulators: Generating Realistic User Personas for Robust Evaluation of LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in settings where they interact with a wide variety of people, including users who are unclear, impatient, or reluctant to share information. However, collecting real interaction data at scale remains expensive. The field has turned to LLM-based user simulators as stand-ins, but these simulators inherit the behavior of their underlying models: cooperative and homogeneous. As a result, agents that appear strong in simulation often fail under the unseen, diverse communication patterns of real users. To narrow this gap, we introduce Persona Policies (PPol), a plug-and-play control layer that induces realistic behavioral variation in user simulators while preserving the original task goals. Rather than hand-crafting personas, we cast persona generation as an LLM-driven evolutionary program search that optimizes a Python generator to discover behaviors and translate them into task-preserving roleplay policies. Candidate generators are guided by a multi-objective fitness score combining human-likeness with broad coverage of human behavioral patterns. Once optimized, the generator produces a diverse population of human-like personas for any task in the domain. Across tau^2-bench retail and airline domains, evolved PPol programs yield 33-62% absolute gains in fitness score over the baseline simulator. In a blinded evaluation, annotators rated PPol-conditioned users as human 80.4% of the time, close to real human traces and nearly twice as frequently as baseline simulators. Agents trained with PPol are more robust to challenging, out-of-distribution behaviors, improving task success by +17% relative to training only on existing simulated interactions. This offers a novel approach to strengthen simulator-based evaluation and training without changing tasks or rewards.

analysis.tldr: 提出Persona Policies (PPol)方法，通过进化程序搜索生成多样化、类人的用户角色，显著提升LLM Agent评估的鲁棒性。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有LLM Agent用户模拟器行为单一且过于合作，导致在模拟中表现良好的Agent在真实用户多样化的交互模式中失败，而收集大规模真实交互数据成本高昂。
analysis.problem: 如何生成具有真实行为多样性的用户模拟器，以更准确地评估和训练LLM Agent。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出基线模拟器继承底层模型的合作与同质行为，导致Agent在模拟中表现强但在真实用户面前失败。
analysis.method: 提出Persona Policies (PPol)，一种即插即用的控制层，通过LLM驱动的进化程序搜索优化Python生成器，发现行为并转化为保持任务目标的角色扮演策略，使用多目标适应度评分（结合人类相似度和行为覆盖度）指导搜索。
contributions: 提出PPol方法，自动生成多样化、类人的用户角色，无需手工设计。
在零售和航空领域实验中，PPol在适应度评分上比基线提升33-62%，人类评估中80.4%被判定为人类，接近真实用户。
使用PPol训练的Agent在挑战性分布外行为上任务成功率相对提升17%。
analysis.experiments: 在tau^2-bench零售和航空领域进行实验，包括适应度评分对比、人类盲评以及Agent鲁棒性测试。
limitations: 可能依赖特定领域任务，泛化性需进一步验证。
进化搜索的计算成本可能较高。
